title: Arrays, Traversals & Optimal Subarray Scans
description: Master array memory addressing mathematics, Kadane's Maximum Subarray Sum algorithm, and two-pointer boundary optimizations.
learningObjectives: Formulate memory addressing calculations for standard 1D and 2D arrays.
Derive and code Kadane's Algorithm to find maximum subarray sums in O(n) runtime.
Optimize nested boundary scans from O(n^2) to O(n) using the two-pointer technique.

quiz.question: Which of the following describes Row-Major Order linearized mapping address calculation?
What is the time complexity of Kadane's Algorithm?
Why does the Two Pointers target-sum technique require the input array to be sorted?
options: So that pointer increments and decrements logically increase or decrease the current sum.
Because arrays cannot be traversed from the end otherwise.
quiz.answer: So that pointer increments and decrements logically increase or decrease the current sum.
